title: citrus medica f. tamurana
intro: Citrus medica f. tamurana, also known as the Buddha's hand or citron, is a variety of citrus fruit that belongs to the Rutaceae family. This plant is known for its unique shape and branches, which are often used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The peel and pulp of the citron contain limonoids and flavonoids, which have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Citrus medica f. tamurana include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and provide antioxidant properties, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
